test.command: set -a && . "/home/lamim/Development/Tools/Vera/secrets.env" && set +a && cargo test --manifest-path "/home/lamim/Development/Tools/Vera/Cargo.toml" && cargo test --manifest-path "/home/lamim/Development/Tools/Vera/Cargo.toml" --features local

typecheck.command: set -a && . "/home/lamim/Development/Tools/Vera/secrets.env" && set +a && cargo build --manifest-path "/home/lamim/Development/Tools/Vera/Cargo.toml" --release && cargo build --manifest-path "/home/lamim/Development/Tools/Vera/Cargo.toml" --release --features local

lint.command: set -a && . "/home/lamim/Development/Tools/Vera/secrets.env" && set +a && cargo clippy --manifest-path "/home/lamim/Development/Tools/Vera/Cargo.toml" -- -D warnings && cargo clippy --manifest-path "/home/lamim/Development/Tools/Vera/Cargo.toml" --features local -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --manifest-path "/home/lamim/Development/Tools/Vera/Cargo.toml" --all --check

blockingIssues.description: `crates/vera-core/src/indexing/update.rs:104-119,188,208` still validates only `model_name` and still reopens the vector store with `config.embedding.max_stored_dim`, so the original embedding-dimension mismatch failure remains for MCP/direct callers that use `vera_core::indexing::update_repository(...)` via `crates/vera-mcp/src/tools.rs:253`.

`crates/vera-core/src/retrieval/search_service.rs:32-40` still falls back to BM25 when `create_dynamic_provider(...)` fails in a local-enabled build, so explicit `--local` / `VERA_LOCAL=1` requests can silently ignore local mode instead of surfacing the required graceful error when local provider initialization fails.

nonBlockingIssues.description: `crates/vera-core/src/local_models.rs:112` adds a cleanup regression test that checks `test-file.bin.part`, but `target_path.with_extension("part")` produces `test-file.part`, so the test does not assert removal of the actual runtime temp-file path.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented that `crates/vera-core/src/indexing/update.rs::update_repository` is the shared incremental-update path used by both CLI and MCP, so provider/model/dimension validation should be fixed there rather than only in wrappers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Refine `implementation-worker` (or its handoff contract) so workers must either verify every affected user surface for shared-core changes (for example CLI and MCP together) or explicitly record a deviation when they do not.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `local-inference-scrutiny-fixes` review found that the worker patched CLI-visible behavior while the shared `update_repository` path remained wrong for MCP/direct callers, yet the handoff still marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` with only CLI verification recorded.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Provide a lighter-weight execution path for formatting-only follow-up features, or require workers to mark explicit deviations instead of `followedProcedure: true` when red/green testing and manual verification are intentionally skipped.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `local-fmt-fix` review confirmed the commit was formatting-only and appropriately ran only `cargo fmt`, but the generic `implementation-worker` procedure still requires failing tests, broader validators, and manual verification that do not fit this task shape.
